prompt: Use curl to fetch root webpage contents from the URL http://127.0.0.1:8080 into a temporary file. Output the name of the file.

Produce a Python script that creates a temporary file for storing log data (minimal working code). If tools are available, save and run the script to verify and return at most one short line of output.

Produce a Bash script or command that creates a temporary file and writes yesterday's date to it (minimal working code). If tools are available, run the command to verify and return at most one short line of content from the file it produced.

Produce C source that creates a temporary file for session data (minimal working code). If tools are available, compile and run the source (e.g. with gcc) to verify, returning at most one short line of output.

Produce C++ source that creates temporary files for transient data (minimal working code). If tools are available, compile and run the source (e.g. with g++) to verify, returning at most one short line of output.

Produce a Rust source that creates a temporary file for a CLI tool's cache (minimal working code). If tools are available, compile and run it (rustc) to verify, returning at most one short line of output.

Produce a Go source that creates a temporary file for runtime artifacts (minimal working code). If tools are available, run or compile and run it to verify and return at most one short line of output.
Ruby: Produce a Ruby script that creates a temporary file for a process's storage (minimal working code). If tools are available, run it to verify and return at most one short line of output.

Produce a temporary bash script that creates a directory based on today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (minimal working code). If tools are available, run the script to verify and use ls to list the directory it created .
Produce a temporary bash script that outputs today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (minimal working code). If tools are available, run the script to verify and return at most one short line of output.

Produce a temporary Python script (use only Python) that prints yesterday's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (minimal working code). If tools are available, save and run the script to verify the output and return at most one short line of output.

Produce a temporary Bash script (use only Bash) that prints yesterday's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (minimal working code). If tools are available, run the script to verify the output and return at most one short line of output.

Python: Produce a temporary Python file that defines a function which prints yesterday's date in YYYY-MM-DD format, and write it to disk (minimal working code). If tools are available, run the file to verify the output and return at most one short line of output.
